title: Confirm only using UTF8 when interacting with wsl.exe
body:
### Feature request description

This is a copy of this issue: https://github.com/podman-desktop/podman-desktop/issues/12998

Currently, the wsl.exe CLI uses UTF16 by default https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13093. We are investigating whether we can change wsl.exe to be UTF8 by default, and want to make sure this will not break folks interacting with it.

So our recommendation is that you set the environment variable WSL_UTF8=1 when interacting with the WSL CLI to get that UTF8 behaviour now, so that you aren't broken in the future.

### Suggest potential solution

I looked through the repo and didn't see any cases of WSL_UTF8 being set. I also searched for `wsl.exe` but didn't see any instance besides a test (wutil_test.go) referencing it. So I wanted to open this issue to make sure it was tracked and known, thank you!

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/1a3b35673d7661fc80b7fbff74d93bac9d53e083/pkg/machine/wsl/machine.go#L97-L114

That seems to be parsing UTF16 so this needs to be changed, I see no reason we we couldn't do that.
 matchOutputLine() in pkg/machine/wsl/wutil/wutil.go as well

cc @l0rd 

--- 

if you want to search for wsl calls look for `"wsl"` because we don't add the .exe suffix when executing it.
This was indeed a problem as the command `podman machine start` fails if `WSL_UTF8=1`. I have opened a PR to enforce UTF-8 encoded output and fix the `podman machine init` problem.
